method_atom: an adaptive-bias exploration algorithm that estimates the effective potential (free energy) over a chosen low-dimensional set of collective coordinates by progressively depositing a history-dependent penalty on already-visited regions, with the deposition amplitude tempered — scaled down as a region accumulates bias — so the added bias smoothly converges to a controlled fraction of the underlying potential; a single tuning parameter interpolates between full flattening of the landscape and unbiased sampling as limiting cases, yielding rigorously controllable convergence and error and concentrating computational effort on the relevant regions of coordinate space.
object_atom: the conformational free-energy landscape of a molecular system as a function of selected molecular order parameters, demonstrated by reconstructing the free-energy surface of the alanine dipeptide molecule.
brief_reason: The tempered, history-dependent adaptive-biasing/convergence machinery is a domain-neutral estimation-and-sampling principle that transfers to any high-dimensional landscape, while the molecular conformations and the alanine dipeptide are the chemistry-specific object it is applied to.
